introduction: Deficiencies of minerals (iron, iodine, zinc, calcium, phosphorus, magnesium, copper, potassium, sodium, chloride, fluoride etc); folic acid and vitamins, are prevalent globally with high rates in under-developed countries, especially sub-Saharan Africa.

features: Zinc, copper: manifestations of enzyme and insulin deficiencies
Phosphorus and fluoride: teeth and bone abnormalities
Vitamin A: keratomalacia, corneal xerosis, night blindness
Vitamin B2 (riboflavin): scrotal and vulval dermatoses, angular stomatitis, scars, Magenta tongue, cheilosis
Vitamin B3 (niacin): scarlet and dry tongue, pellagra
Vitamin C (Ascorbic acid): scurvy, petechiae and musculo-skeletal haemorrhages
Vitamin D: rickets, epiphyseal enlargement, muscle wasting, bossing of skull bone, 'thoracic rosary', persistently open anterior fontanelle, genu valgum or varum

prevention: Adequate intake of locally available, nutritious foods
